results.title: Matt Zagursky
highlights:
Head of AI at [Erie Insurance Group](https://www.linkedin.com/company/erie-insurance-group)
...
I lead AI at Erie Insurance, where my focus is turning AI from scattered experiments into durable enterprise capability: clear strategy, governed delivery, measurable value, and adoption across real business operations. My background sits at the intersection of AI, data, enterprise technology, and business transformation. Over the last 16 years, I have built and scaled technology-enabled businesses, led AI and data initiatives for major brands, and operated across strategy, product, engineering, operations, and executive leadership. Before Erie, I founded and scaled Novo, an AI-enabled creator and growth platform serving enterprise clients including Microsoft and Amazon. I personally led the development of Nexus, our proprietary AI platform built on one of the largest private influencer datasets in the market. Nexus helped reduce spend inefficiency by 67% and supported 300% annual growth by turning fragmented market data into faster, better decisions. Earlier in my career, I led large-scale scientific data infrastructure work for Pan-STARRS1, helping design and direct systems built to process more than 1.6 petabytes of astronomical data. That experience shaped how I think about modern AI: the hard part is rarely the model alone. The hard part is the operating system around it: data, governance, security, reliability, adoption, measurement, and executive alignment. I am strongest where strategy and execution have to meet. I help organizations decide where AI can create real advantage, where it introduces unacceptable risk, and how to move from pilots to production without losing control of cost, quality, compliance, or trust. My work now is focused on enterprise AI transformation in insurance: building the strategy, operating model, governance, and delivery discipline required to make AI useful, safe, measurable, and scalable inside a highly regulated company.
...
### Head of AI at [Erie Insurance Group](https://www.linkedin.com/company/erie-insurance-group) (Current)
...
Lead enterprise technology and AI transformation for Erie Insurance, focused on modernizing the operating model required to deliver business and technology change at scale. The role spans IT operating model redesign, executive alignment, end-to-end team strategy, platform team evolution, enterprise AI enablement, governance, prioritization, delivery discipline, and value measurement inside a large, regulated insurance carrier. Current focus areas include helping shift technology toward a more product and platform oriented model: clearer ownership, stronger alignment to business outcomes, reusable enterprise capabilities, self-service platforms, better intake and prioritization, and operating rhythms that improve speed, accountability, and scale. My work sits at the intersection of enterprise technology strategy, organizational transformation, AI, platforms, and executive operating discipline. The goal is to help technology become a more integrated, outcome-driven transformation engine for the business.
...
- Architected and led the end-to-end development of "Nexus," a proprietary AI-powered management platform that transformed the company from a services firm into a technology-driven market leader, achieving 300% annual growth.

results.title: Staff Data Scientist (Bodily Injury Claims) | Hirevector
highlights:
Staff Data Scientist (Bodily Injury Claims) | Hirevector
...
# Staff Data Scientist (Bodily Injury Claims)
...
- We’re seeking a Staff Data Scientist to architect and lead the development of our core Claim Clustering Platform—the intelligence layer that powers how we compare and analyze Bodily Injury (BI) claims across Workers’ Compensation, General Liability, and Auto Liability. This is a highly technical, hands-on role where you will design systems that move beyond experimentation into robust, production-grade solutions. You’ll collaborate closely with Machine Learning Engineers and MLOps specialists to ensure scalability, reliability, and continuous improvement. You’ll also serve as a critical bridge to Data and Application Engineering teams, ensuring seamless integration with downstream systems such as benchmarking and fraud detection.
...
- Design and own the mathematical and technical framework for a scalable clustering engine that groups claims based on clinical, legal, and financial attributes.
...
- Partner with Machine Learning Engineers to translate prototypes into optimized production systems, and work with MLOps to implement automated retraining, monitoring, and model lifecycle management.
...
- Collaborate with Data and Application Engineering teams to define APIs and data contracts that power internal tools such as attorney and physician benchmarking, as well as fraud detection systems.
...
- Develop advanced representations of claims using both structured data (e.g., ICD codes, geographic data, indemnity and legal costs) and unstructured data (e.g., adjuster notes, medical records, legal documents).
...
- At least 7 years in Data Science with a strong track record of deploying models into production environments.
...
- Deep experience with Bodily Injury claims, including 4+ years of hands-on work in Workers’ Compensation or General Liability. Familiarity with claim lifecycles, medical billing (ICD/CPT), litigation processes, and reserve dynamics is crucial.
...
- Strong proficiency in Python and solid software engineering fundamentals, including system design, CI/CD pipelines, and API development/versioning.
...
- Expertise in unsupervised learning techniques (clustering, dimensionality reduction) and NLP methods (transformers, embeddings, LLM-based approaches) for analyzing complex, unstructured data.

results.title: Associate Data Scientist | Hirequorum
highlights:
Associate Data Scientist | Hirequorum
...
# Associate Data Scientist
...
### Associate Data Scientist
...
The Insurance Research Council (IRC) Associate Data Scientist is responsible for conducting primary research and creating content in support of the IRC’s public policy mission and member value proposition. This role requires not only strong analytical skills, but also good communication skills (writing and presenting) to translate abstract mathematical and statistical concepts into everyday language.
...
### What You’ll Do:
...
- Utilize advanced analytical techniques (including predictive modeling, machine learning, and AI) to conduct original research in the IRC proprietary auto injury claim analytics database (AICAD)

- Analyze insurance, economic, financial, and survey data to generate original research to benefit the insurance industry, IRC members, policymakers, and consumers

- Assist in the acquisition, preparation, analysis, and presentation of insurance, economic, financial, and survey data, including future updates of AICAD

- Perform analysis by processing data using best practices in data governance

- Communicate data excellence and insights via traditional reporting products and data visualization tools

- Contribute to the production of key IRC research projects, including reports, summaries, and presentations

- Perform additional data analytics and administrative tasks, as assigned
...
### What We’re Looking For:
...
- Bachelor’s degree required

- Graduate degree in Mathematics, Statistics, or related field strongly preferred

- Experience with programming languages required (SAS, R, SQL/VBA, Python)

- Experience with AWS cloud-based data hosting environment and analytical software packages like Sagemaker strongly preferred

- 2+ years of work experience in analytics strongly preferred

- Must have 2+ years of experience conducting quantitative research

- Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite required (Word, PowerPoint, Outlook). Advanced Microsoft Excel skills required

- Prior experience and/or working knowledge of the risk management or insurance industry is preferred
...
### Key Competencies
...
- Technical Knowledge:

- Experience working in AWS cloud-based data hosting environment and analytical software packages like Sagemaker

- Experience with programming languages (e.g., SAS, R, SQL/VBA, Python)

- Advanced Microsoft Excel skills

- Research & Data Analysis: Quantitative research and analysis skills - tools, methodologies, database management; Strong attention to detail and accuracy

- Communication skills: Exceptional oral and written communication skills and ability to communicate throughout all levels of the organization, both internally and externally; Ability to present information and respond to questions professionally and effectively, conversationally and in written communications
...
- Collaboration: Experience working in a team-oriented, collaborative environment; Exceptional interpersonal skills, with a focus on rapport-building, listening, and questioning skills.

My approach blends analytics and strategy to ensure that data not only tells a story but also directly impacts the bottom line. Throughout my career, I’ve had the opportunity to lead high-impact projects and streamline data processes for Erie Insurance Group. Some key achievements include: • Developed and modeled data warehouse programs using SQL and SAS, improving business decision-making and KPI performance. • Automated ad-hoc and recurring reports using Power BI, reducing manual data manipulation and boosting reporting efficiency. • Analyzed financial data to provide actionable recommendations, resulting in an improvement in forecasted revenue growth for FY 2024. My passion for data analysis and excellence in strategic reporting fuels my drive to continually push the boundaries of what is possible in business. results.title: Data Scientist, GenAI - Atlanta | Great Insurance Jobs
highlights:
Data Scientist, GenAI - Atlanta | Great Insurance Jobs
...
# Data Scientist, GenAI
...
At AIG, we are making long-term investments in a brand-new, innovative Generative AI team, designed to explore new possibilities for how artificial intelligence can be applied in insurance and beyond, and we need your help.
...
With the support and investment needed to explore new frontiers in generative AI, you'll be working alongside talented colleagues, innovating and leading projects that will transform how we manage risk and serve our customers.
...
central to our
...
We will incorporate best-in
...
class engineering and product management
...
the opportunity to
...
we'd love to hear from
...
As a Data Scientist at AIG, you will serve a critical role in the architecture, development, and delivery of impactful enterprise-level Generative AI solutions. We are seeking a highly technical individual contributor with extensive hands-on experience, as we are an applied data science team operating in an agile and production-oriented environment. You will be focused on Large Language Models (LLMs) and Machine Learning (ML), acting as a primary technical driver for our most complex projects.
...
End-to-End Development: Lead the development and successful delivery of data science and generative AI solutions in accordance with business requirements.
...
Production Oversight: Monitor solutions in production to ensure performance, reliability, and accuracy.
...
Technical Collaboration: Partner with cross-functional teams including product managers, engineers, and business leaders to translate requirements into technical reality.
...
Evaluation & Quality: Build robust evaluation frameworks to measure LLM efficacy, manage ground truth dataset quality, and guide the product development roadmap.
...
Architecture: Design scalable ML pipelines and RAG frameworks that integrate seamlessly with enterprise data structures.
...
5+ years of experience in a data science role, with a strong emphasis on NLP and ML, working in an agile production-oriented environment.
...
Proven Delivery: Experience successfully delivering multiple GenAI, analytical, or ML projects from conception through to production.
...
Python Expertise: Strong, expert-level proficiency in Python and its data science ecosystem (e.g., PyTorch, Pandas, Scikit-learn).
...
LLM Mastery: 5+ years of practical experience with open-source Large Language Models (Llama 3, Mixtral, etc.), including prompt engineering, inference optimization, and fine-tuning.
...
GenAI Solutions: 3+ years of experience building Generative AI Solutions, including designing and building RAG frameworks, validation pipelines, observability, and monitoring solutions.
...
Education: Master's degree in data science, Computer Science, or a related quantitative field.
...
Palantir Platform Experience: Hands-on experience or certification in the Palantir platform (Foundry/AIP).
...
Ontology Mastery: A strong understanding of Ontology—specifically how to map complex real-world data entities and relationships into a digital twin framework to power AI applications.
...
A "hands-on" mindset with the desire to be a primary technical lead through code quality and architectural excellence.
...

results.title: Inside Claims Representative I | Jobflarely
highlights:
Inside Claims Representative I | Jobflarely
...
# Inside Claims Representative I
...
Posted Jun 19, 2026
...
At Erie Insurance, you’re not just part of a Fortune 500 company; you’re also a valued member of a diverse and inclusive team that includes more than 6,000 employees and over 13,000 independent agencies. Our Employees work in the Home Office complex located in Erie, PA, and in our Field Offices that span 12 states and the District of Columbia.
...
- Position Summary
...
Under close supervision, handles liability and property claims within designated authority. Resolves coverage and/or liability issues in accordance with applicable state insurance laws, regulations, and procedures.
...
There are two job openings
...
The successful candidate will work remotely of the Cary Branch Office.
...
### Duties and Responsibilities
...
Investigates and adjudicates claims within designated authority, ensuring compliance with appropriate statutory laws. Obtains additional information as required to determine liability. Documents claim files and facilitates processing of claims in collaboration with other departments. highlights: /ML Engineer to join their Data & AI team, where the role involves designing, building, testing, and deploying production-grade Generative AI solutions and traditional machine learning models. Responsibilities Build and iterate on LLM-powered features (e.g., chat, summarization, extraction, classification) using approved model providers and internal platforms Design retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) solutions, including document ingestion, chunking, embeddings, vector search, and prompt templates Develop and evaluate prompts, tools, and agentic workflows; implement guardrails (grounding, citations, refusal behavior) and improve reliability Create offline and online evaluation for GenAI systems (test sets, automated metrics, human review processes) and track quality over time Assist with developing, training, and evaluating traditional ML models when needed (e.g., classification, regression, anomaly detection, recommendation) Clean, preprocess, and analyze structured and unstructured data; build reproducible data pipelines for model inputs and evaluation Collaborate with data scientists, software engineers, security, and product teams to translate business requirements into safe, scalable solutions Support deployment, monitoring, and cost/performance optimization for GenAI services (latency, token usage, caching, drift, incident response) Write clean, maintainable, and well-documented code; contribute to code reviews, runbooks, and user documentation Works under guidance to make day-to-day technical decisions such as selecting baseline prompts/RAG patterns, defining evaluation criteria (quality, safety, latency, cost), and proposing experiment plans Escalates architecture, production rollout, security, and data governance decisions to senior engineers/tech lead; contributes input through code reviews, documentation, and demos Skills Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related field (or equivalent practical experience) Internship, academic project, or capstone experience building GenAI/LLM or ML applications Hands-on experience with at least one LLM framework/tooling (e.g., LangChain, LlamaIndex, Semantic Kernel) or equivalent custom orchestration Exposure to RAG building blocks (document ingestion, chunking, embeddings, vector databases/search) Familiarity with evaluation approaches for LLM systems (golden sets, qualitative rubrics, automated checks) and basic experiment tracking Familiarity with SQL and relational databases; experience working with unstructured text data is a plus Basic knowledge of cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP) and deploying services via APIs Experience with version control tools (Git/GitHub) and collaborative development workflows IT Skills: Python; Jupyter/VS Code; Git; Linux fundamentals; Docker basics; REST APIs; SQL; basic CI/CD concepts; familiarity with LLM APIs and orchestration frameworks (e.g., LangChain/LlamaIndex/Semantic Kernel); embeddings and vector databases/search (e.g., Pinecone/FAISS/Azure AI Search or equivalent); experiment tracking/evaluation tooling (e.g., MLflow or equivalent); basic monitoring/observability concepts (logs, traces, latency, token usage) Benefits Medical, dental, and vision insurance 401(k) retirement plan with a company match that vests fully on day one Eight (8) weeks of paid parental leave after just three (3) months of employment Paid time off policy that includes vacation time, personal time, sick time, floating holidays, and eleven (11) company holidays Flexible spending and health savings accounts Life and AD&D insurance Short- and long-term disability coverage Legal assistance Supplemental plans such as pet, critical illness, accident, and hospital indemnity insurance Voluntary commuter benefits Family planning and care resources Well-being initiatives Peer-to-peer recognition programs Company Overview Legend Biotech is a biopharmaceutical company that develops and commercializes novel cell therapies for

The Sr Staff AI Scientist - Generative AI will apply knowledge and experience to real world problems and seek to utilize their skills reduce the cost of healthcare and improve health quality and outcomes. With access to dedicated on premise and cloud based big data solutions, the team can work with a vast amount of structured and unstructured data including claims, membership, physician demographics, medical records and others to begin to solve some of the most pressing healthcare issues of our time. A Data Scientist at Cotiviti will be given the opportunity to work directly with a team of healthcare professionals including analysts, clinicians, coding specialists, auditors and innovators to set aggressive goals and execute on them with the team. ...
Lead model deployment for experimentation, implementing A/B testing, monitoring model performance, and iterating on solutions based on empirical results.
...
Work closely with AI engineers, software developers, product managers, and other data scientists to deliver end-to-end AI-enabled solutions.
...
